questions.text: The plant that feeds and traps insects is -
questions.explanation: Drosera is an insectivorous (carnivorous) plant that traps and digests insects.

questions.text: The green pigment in the leaves is called -
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is the green pigment that traps sunlight for photosynthesis.

questions.text: Which one of the following is a parasite?
questions.explanation: Dodder (Amarbel) is a parasitic plant that uses sucking roots (haustoria) to absorb food from a host.

questions.text: Rhizobium is a good example of -
questions.explanation: Rhizobium lives symbiotically in root nodules of legumes; both partners benefit.

questions.text: Cuscuta is an example of -
questions.explanation: Cuscuta (Amarbel) is a parasitic plant that depends on a host plant for food.

questions.text: Autotrophic nutrition is found only in -
questions.explanation: Green plants make their own food by photosynthesis, so autotrophic nutrition is found in plants.

questions.text: The plant that feeds and traps insects is -
questions.explanation: The Venus-fly trap is an insectivorous plant that captures and digests insects.

questions.text: Association of two different organisms in which both are benefited is called -
questions.explanation: Symbiosis is a partnership between two organisms where both benefit, e.g. lichen.

questions.text: The balance of CO2 and O2 in the atmosphere is maintained due to -
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis uses CO2 and releases O2, maintaining the atmospheric balance.

questions.text: Animals and plants do not depend on plants for their food.
questions.explanation: False. All animals depend on plants directly or indirectly for food.

questions.text: Omnivores eat only flesh.
questions.explanation: False. Omnivores eat both plants and the flesh of other animals.

questions.text: Sucking roots are called haustoria.
questions.explanation: True. Parasitic plants like Dodder use special sucking roots called haustoria.

questions.text: The living organism from which a parasite derives its food is called the host.
questions.explanation: True. The organism that provides food to a parasite is the host.

questions.text: Amarbel (Dodder) is an example of a -
questions.explanation: Amarbel depends on a host plant for nutrition, so it is a parasite.

questions.text: Rhizobium is associated with which mode of nutrition?
questions.explanation: Rhizobium and the legume plant live in symbiosis, benefiting each other.

questions.text: Mushroom obtains its nutrition as a -
questions.explanation: Mushrooms (fungi) feed on dead organic matter, so they are saprophytes.

questions.text: Drosera is an example of a -
questions.explanation: Drosera traps and digests insects, so it is an insectivorous plant.

questions.text: Green plants are an example of -
questions.explanation: Green plants make their own food by photosynthesis, so they are autotrophs.

questions.text: Pitcher plant and ____ are insectivorous plants.
questions.explanation: Pitcher plant and Drosera (and Venus-fly trap) are insectivorous plants.

questions.text: Green plants use carbon dioxide, water and ____ to make food.
questions.explanation: Green plants need carbon dioxide, water and sunlight (with chlorophyll) to make food.

questions.text: Lichen is the mutual combination of an alga and a ____.
questions.explanation: A lichen is a symbiotic combination of an alga and a fungus.

questions.text: Dodder is an example of a ____.
questions.explanation: Dodder (Amarbel) depends on a host for food, so it is a parasite.
